title: SA-EMO: Structure-Aligned Encoder Mixture of Operators for Generalizable Full-waveform Inversion
problem: Existing data-driven FWI models exhibit inconsistent cross-type generalization and degraded high-frequency reconstruction under complex geological conditions due to temporal-spatial and structural misalignment between the waveform domain and the velocity domain.

solution.name: SA-EMO (Structure-Aligned Encoder-Mixture-of-Operators) framework

components.name: Structure-aligned encoder
components.description: Learns a physically consistent projection from raw wavefields to a latent space that aligns with the target velocity structure.

components.name: Four complementary neural operator experts
components.description: FNO, WNO, MNO, and LNO jointly capture global, multiscale, hierarchical, and local physical priors within this latent space.

components.name: Adaptive routing mechanism
components.description: Fuses experts via type-weighted and group-weighted aggregation with strong-weak activation, dynamically balancing dominant and auxiliary experts to adapt to different geological regimes.

solution.training: The entire system is trained with a hybrid spatial-spectral loss for unified optimization.

results.robustness: Strong-weak routing enhances robustness across all ten sub-datasets and preserves structural continuity on the Marmousi2 dataset, demonstrating strong out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization.

contributions: Proposed a structure-aligned encoder that explicitly aligns waveform and velocity domains, achieving balanced spectral energy and improved single-operator accuracy.
Introduced an operator-level mixture-of-experts that integrates FNO, WNO, MNO, and LNO to jointly capture global, multiscale, and local physical priors in a unified latent space.
Designed a strong-weak activation routing mechanism within a hierarchical type- and group-weighted framework, improving cross-type generalization and interpretability without explicit type labels.
Conducted extensive experiments on ten OpenFWI sub-datasets and the OOD Marmousi2 dataset, showing that the encoder improves accuracy while routing enhances generalization.
